title_en: As-San'ani and His Commentary on Bulugh al-Maram
content_en:
Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Amir as-San'ani (1099–1182 AH / 1688–1768 CE) was a leading Yemeni hadith scholar and jurist of the twelfth Islamic century whose works reflect a combination of deep traditional learning and critical independence. He was born in San'a, Yemen, into a scholarly family, and received an extensive education in the Islamic sciences, eventually becoming one of the most prolific scholars of his era. He is known for combining mastery of hadith methodology with a critical approach to taqlid (blind adherence to legal schools) that anticipated and influenced later Islamic reform movements.

As-San'ani's most widely used work is Subul as-Salam Sharh Bulugh al-Maram (Pathways of Peace: Commentary on the Attainment of the Objective), a commentary on Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani's famous hadith anthology Bulugh al-Maram min Adillat al-Ahkam. Bulugh al-Maram was specifically compiled by Ibn Hajar as a concise selection of the hadiths most frequently cited as evidence for legal rulings across the major schools of Islamic law, with attribution of each hadith to its source and brief notes on chain quality.

As-San'ani's commentary on Bulugh al-Maram became the standard teaching commentary on this enormously popular text. Bulugh al-Maram itself became one of the most widely taught hadith texts in the world — used from Morocco to Indonesia as a foundational legal-hadith reference — and as-San'ani's commentary provided the explanatory framework that students needed to understand the hadiths and their legal implications.

Subul as-Salam is organized to follow Bulugh al-Maram hadith by hadith, providing explanation of each narration's text, analysis of its chain and authentication status, presentation of the legal positions derived from it across the major schools, and as-San'ani's own critical evaluation of the competing positions. The commentary is accessible without being superficial, making it ideal as a teaching text at the intermediate level of Islamic studies. As-San'ani's influence on the eighteenth-century revival of hadith-based legal reasoning in Yemen and beyond was substantial, and Subul as-Salam, as his most widely used work, was the primary vehicle through which his methodology reached students across the Islamic world. Ash-Shawkani, his most distinguished successor, built directly on the foundation as-San'ani had established.
title_id: As-Shan'ani dan Syarahnya atas Bulugh al-Maram
content_id:
Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Amir as-Shan'ani (1099-1182 H / 1688-1768 M) adalah seorang ahli hadis dan ahli hukum Yaman terkemuka pada abad kedua belas Islam yang karya-karyanya mencerminkan perpaduan antara keilmuan tradisional yang mendalam dan kemandirian kritis. Ia lahir di Sanaa, Yaman, dari keluarga ulama, dan mendapatkan pendidikan yang luas dalam ilmu-ilmu Islam, akhirnya menjadi salah satu ulama paling produktif di eranya. Ia dikenal karena memadukan penguasaan metodologi hadis dengan pendekatan kritis terhadap taklid yang mengantisipasi dan mempengaruhi gerakan-gerakan pembaruan Islam belakangan.

Karya as-Shan'ani yang paling banyak digunakan adalah Subul as-Salam Syarh Bulugh al-Maram (Jalan-Jalan Kedamaian: Komentar atas Bulugh al-Maram), sebuah syarah atas antologi hadis terkenal karya Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, Bulugh al-Maram min Adillat al-Ahkam. Bulugh al-Maram secara khusus disusun oleh Ibn Hajar sebagai pilihan ringkas hadis-hadis yang paling sering dikutip sebagai dalil untuk hukum-hukum fikih dari berbagai mazhab Islam besar, dengan pencantuman sumber setiap hadis dan catatan singkat tentang kualitas sanad.

Syarah as-Shan'ani atas Bulugh al-Maram menjadi syarah pengajaran standar untuk teks yang sangat populer ini. Bulugh al-Maram sendiri menjadi salah satu teks hadis yang paling banyak diajarkan di dunia, digunakan dari Maroko hingga Indonesia sebagai referensi hadis-hukum yang mendasar, dan syarah as-Shan'ani menyediakan kerangka penjelasan yang dibutuhkan para pelajar untuk memahami hadis-hadis beserta implikasi hukumnya.

Subul as-Salam disusun mengikuti Bulugh al-Maram hadis per hadis, menyajikan penjelasan teks setiap riwayat, analisis sanad dan status otentikasinya, pemaparan posisi hukum yang diturunkan darinya di berbagai mazhab, dan evaluasi kritis as-Shan'ani sendiri atas posisi-posisi yang bersaing. Syarah ini mudah dipahami tanpa bersifat dangkal, menjadikannya ideal sebagai teks pengajaran pada tingkat menengah studi Islam. Pengaruh as-Shan'ani pada kebangkitan kembali penalaran hukum berbasis hadis di abad kedelapan belas di Yaman dan sekitarnya sangat besar, dan Subul as-Salam, sebagai karyanya yang paling banyak digunakan, merupakan wahana utama yang melaluinya metodologinya menjangkau para pelajar di seluruh dunia Islam. Asy-Syaukani, penerusnya yang paling terkemuka, membangun langsung di atas fondasi yang telah as-Shan'ani tegakkan.
content_ar: محمد ﷺ ibn Ismail al-Amir as-San'ani (1099–1182 AH / 1688–1768 CE) was a leading Yemeni الحديث scholar and jurist of the twelfth Islamic century whose works reflect a combination of deep التقليدي learning and البالغ الأهمية independence. He was born in San'a, Yemen, into a العلمي الأسرة, and received an extensive education in the Islamic sciences, eventually becoming one of the most prolific العلماء of his era. He is known for combining mastery of الحديث methodology with a البالغ الأهمية approach to taqlid (blind adherence to legal schools) that anticipated and influenced later Islamic reform movements. As-San'ani's most widely used work is Subul as-Salam Sharh Bulugh al-Maram (Pathways of Peace: Commentary on the Attainment of the Objective), a commentary on Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani's famous الحديث anthology Bulugh al-Maram min Adillat al-Ahkam. Bulugh al-Maram was specifically compiled by Ibn Hajar as a concise selection of the hadiths most frequently cited as evidence for legal rulings across the major schools of Islamic law, with attribution of each الحديث to its source and brief notes on chain quality. As-San'ani's commentary on Bulugh al-Maram became the standard teaching commentary on this enormously popular text. Bulugh al-Maram itself became one of the most widely taught الحديث texts in the الدنيا — used from Morocco to Indonesia as a foundational legal-الحديث reference — and as-San'ani's commentary provided the explanatory framework that students needed لفهم the hadiths and their legal implications. Subul as-Salam is organized للاتباع Bulugh al-Maram الحديث by الحديث, providing explanation of each narration's text, analysis of its chain and authentication status, presentation of the legal positions derived from it across the major schools, and as-San'ani's own البالغ الأهمية evaluation of the competing positions. The commentary is accessible without being superficial, making it ideal as a teaching text at the intermediate level of Islamic studies. As-San'ani's influence on the eighteenth-century revival of الحديث-based legal reasoning in Yemen and beyond was substantial, and Subul as-Salam, as his most widely used work, was the الأساسي vehicle through which his methodology reached students across the Islamic الدنيا. Ash-Shawkani, his most distinguished successor, built directly on the foundation as-San'ani had established.
